leadText: Armarouge (Japanese: グレンアルマ Gurenarma) is a dual-type Fire/Psychic Pokémon introduced in Generation IX. It evolves from Charcadet when exposed to a set of Auspicious Armor. It is one of Charcadet's final forms, the other being Ceruledge.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Armarouge using its pauldrons as a cannon Armarouge is a bipedal, humanoid Pokémon. Its body is primarily black, with red colorations on its cylindrical legs and hands, the latter resembling oven mitts. Its head is also red, with yellow sharp "ears" protruding from either side. The top of its head is yellow and has a fire burning on top, resembling the plume on a knight's helmet. Its face is covered with a black mask with three holes at the center, and its eyes have red irises and wisps emanating from its yellow pupils. Armarouge's torso and shoulders are covered in an old set of yellow and red armor , along with levitating large yellow pauldrons over its shoulders. Armarouge's armor is said to have belonged to a distinguished warrior, and is the source of its psychic power. The armor allows Armarouge to control its inner fire energy and use attacks. Armarouge is loyal and believes in fighting fair, willing to fight head-on no matter how strong the foe is. It first focuses on using its sturdy armor for defense before using high firepower in order to take down opponents. When attacking, Armarouge moves its pauldrons to its hands and puts them together to form a cannon, which it uses to shoot blazing projectiles . [ 1 ] It is said to be very loyal to its Trainer , pledging to protect them. However, it is known to repeatedly leave on its own to train and strengthen its psychic energy. Armarouge is the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Armor Cannon . Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Armarouge evolves from Charcadet and is one of its final forms, the other being Ceruledge . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Charcadet Fire → First Evolution Armarouge Fire Psychic ↑ Armarouge | Pokémon Scarlet and Pokémon Violet

This Pokémon is incredibly loyal. Violet This Pokémon clads itself in armor that has been fortified by psychic and fire energy, and it shoots blazing fireballs. Legends: Z-A Though Armarouge pledges loyalty to its Trainer, it repeatedly leaves to go train on its own to strengthen its psychic energy. Spin-off games Pokopia #201 Pokopia Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal. sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Armarouge and its evolutionary relatives were designed by Tomohiro Kitakaze . [ 1 ] Armarouge's name was misspelled as "Arm o rouge" on the official Pokémon Scarlet and Violet website; specifically, in the section describing the move Armor Cannon . [ 2 ] This error has been corrected as of September 13, 2022. [ 3 ] Armarouge has the same base stat values as its counterpart Ceruledge, just assigned to different stats. Origin [ edit | edit source ] Armarouge may be based on a possessed suit of armor. Its design could also be based on medieval heroic tales of European knights, as well as the idea of chivalry . One specific point of origin may be the legend of the Spanish knight Guifré el Pilós , known for his golden armor and shield, on which was said the emperor Charles the Bald painted with his fingers four red lines using the blood of the former, in honor of his bravery. This might explain the red colorations on Armarouge's armor and extremities. Armarouge's ability to put its pauldrons together on its arms to form a cannon is reminiscent of depictions of arm cannons in fictional media. The shape the pauldrons take when in this form also resembles a gas cylinder , a metal vessel that contains pressurized gases for uses such as combustion. The Auspicious Armor required to evolve Charcadet into Armarouge may be inspired by ceremonial cannons cast from captured weapons and scrap metals, or cannons kept as war trophies in early-modern warfare. Name origin [ edit | edit source ] Armarouge may be a combination of arma (Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian for weapon), armadura (Spanish and Portuguese for armor) or armatura (Italian for armor), and rouge (French for red). Gurenarma may be a combination of 紅蓮 guren (crimson) and arma , armadura , or armatura . ↑ Tweet by 北風友裕 (@kitakazeee). Posted on November 17, 2023 (archived). ↑ Earliest archive of Armarouge — Pokémon Scarlet and Pokémon Violet Official Website (September 7, 2022). ↑ Cite error: Invalid tag; no text was provided for refs named SVsite
